title: Patience remains an elusive virtue for Legacy FC attack
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Legacy FC lost 1-0 to San Diego Wave at Gillette Stadium, extending their search for a first NWSL win.
Coach Filipa Patão emphasizes patience, seeking improvements in the attack after four games with only one goal scored.
Forward Amanda Gutierres, despite involvement in build-up play, struggles to convert opportunities, with no goals in four matches.

title: Melrose man charged with stealing hundreds of thousands from clients, prosecutors say
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Henry Rappa Jr., a suspended attorney from Melrose, is charged with embezzling hundreds of thousands of dollars from clients.
Rappa was arraigned on charges including embezzlement, larceny over $1,200, and unauthorized practice of law.
Prosecutors allege Rappa continued practicing law after suspension and misused a client's $660,000 trust funds.

title: Eugene Mirman posts message after fiery NH crash and dramatic rescue
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Eugene Mirman was rescued from a burning vehicle after crashing into the Bedford Toll Plaza in New Hampshire.
The crash left Mirman seriously injured; he was pulled out by a state trooper and two security officers.
No charges have been filed; the crash remains under investigation by New Hampshire State Police.

title: NH trooper assaulted in unprovoked attack
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Jonathan G. Newcomb of Dover charged with assault on NH State Trooper in an unprovoked attack.
Incident occurred in a parking lot off Dover Point Road; both Newcomb and trooper were hospitalized with minor injuries.
Newcomb faces charges including felony criminal threatening, attempted firearm theft, and various misdemeanors.

title: This runner ‘barely finished’ the Boston Marathon last year. Now he’s back to run again.
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Bryan Mallett, 24, from Lynnfield, is running the Boston Marathon to support Mass General for a second consecutive year.
Mallett is inspired by his late second mother, Sharla Sieve, who passed from metastatic colorectal cancer in December 2022.
He aims to honor Sieve's memory by completing all major marathons, with Boston being his most significant due to personal connections.

title: NFL executives reportedly ‘expect’ Patriots to acquire A.J. Brown
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: NFL executives expect the Patriots to acquire Eagles WR A.J. Brown after June 1 for salary-cap reasons.
The Eagles reportedly seek a first-round draft pick and additional picks in exchange for A.J. Brown.
Patriots head coach Mike Vrabel remains non-committal about the potential trade for A.J. Brown.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: '28 Years Later: The Bone Temple' is now streaming on Netflix, directed by Nia DaCosta and features Ralph Fiennes.
'The Testament of Ann Lee', starring Amanda Seyfried, is available on Hulu, chronicling the life of Quaker Ann Lee.
'Ken Burns Presents: Henry David Thoreau' documentary is streaming on PBS, featuring narration by George Clooney.

title: Caleb Durbin’s redemption, Willson Contreras’s swagger & other takeaways from Red Sox’ home-opening win
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Red Sox defeated the Padres 5-2 in their home opener, snapping a five-game losing streak.
Caleb Durbin broke an 0-for-34 slump with an RBI single, receiving cheers from Fenway fans.
Willson Contreras hit a home run and showcased his confidence with an ABS challenge and bat flip.

title: Here are the dates for the Patriots’ offseason workout schedule
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The New England Patriots' offseason workouts start April 20, beginning with conditioning drills and meetings.
OTAs for the Patriots are scheduled on May 27, May 29, June 1-2, June 4, June 8-9, and June 11.
A mandatory minicamp for the Patriots will take place from June 15-17 in Foxborough.

title: Patriots double up on edge rushers in latest ESPN mock draft
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots mock draft suggests picking edge rushers Cashius Howell and Gabe Jacas in the first and second rounds.
Cashius Howell recorded 11.5 sacks last season at Texas A&M and is compared to NFL's Will McDonald IV.
Gabe Jacas, seen as an NFL-ready run defender, improved his sack totals to 11 last season at Illinois.
